parts.label: Fin Set x 4 Display Mock
parts.spec: Primary_structure fin_set display mock with root tabs, alignment_key, bolt_circle_dummy, inspection callouts, no stability/performance claim

parts.label: Avionics Bay Mock Tray
parts.spec: Avionics_bay educational tray with board_standoff, harness_passthrough, connector labels, EMI shield mock, no ordnance or flight computer authority

parts.label: Battery/Power Display Module
parts.spec: Power_display battery_module dummy with access_panel, keyed connector label, service cover, mass budget note, no live energy system

parts.label: Recovery Bay Display Dummy
parts.spec: Recovery_bay educational display dummy with deploy_door, hinge, parachute volume label, inspection window, no deployment charge or flight recovery instruction

requirements: Non-functional stage_engine_mock with bell_nozzle_shell, cutaway_window, engine_display_cartridge slide_rail, educational label only
Engine display cartridge: combustion chamber dummy, bell nozzle shell, turbopump dummy in cutaway bay (non-functional)
Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
Tank, interstage, avionics, recovery, and engine-display modules are labeled educational/display-only
Cutaway shows tank dummies, interstage datum, avionics bay, recovery bay, and removable engine display cartridge
Risk/verification focuses on mockup structure, inspection, interfaces, and unsafe overclaiming

risk.mit: Conduct process simulations and pilot runs to validate manufacturability.

risk.desc: Risk of overclaiming in design documentation implying operational readiness.
risk.mit: Review documentation for any overclaiming and ensure it is educational only.

risk.desc: Potential for uncontrolled tolerance accumulation in datum chain.
risk.mit: Ensure datum chain is continuous and controlled to prevent accumulation.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Assembly order evidence, inspection windows, readiness checklist hooks, connection types and load-path closure

physics_paths.description: Path for visual inspection of assembly datum and checklist callouts
Thermal path from inspection window to assembly datum for heat management
Load path closure marks ensure structural integrity and load transfer
Service access cut for maintenance and inspection

integration_opportunities.description: Provides visual access for inspection
Provides a reference point for assembly and alignment
Provides a checklist for assembly verification

manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures are placed to avoid overhangs and ensure part orientation
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection and non-destructive testing (NDT) for defects

verification_focus.description: Ensure material and process are suitable for the application
Verify that all inspection points are traceable to specific features
Assess risks related to access, interfaces, tolerances, and process limitations
Ensure each requirement is mapped to a specific verification method
Verify that all interfaces are properly controlled and documented
Ensure the flowpath is educational and does not imply operational functionality
Ensure the cutaway shows all necessary internal features
Ensure risk language is safe and does not imply operational readiness
Verify that all stage interfaces have proper datum evidence
Ensure all connection types are named and properly documented
Ensure the assembly sequence is feasible and all parts are accessible
Ensure the datum chain is continuous and does not allow uncontrolled tolerance accumulation
Verify that all load paths are properly closed and supported by appropriate connection types
Ensure the design moves in the right direction for structural development
Ensure the design uses the fewest interfaces necessary for service and manufacture
